title: [SeparateConstOffsetFromGEP] - Fix the simplification of GEP index when its operand is an XOR constant.
author: pawan-nirpal-031
description:
Simplifying GEP indices involving XOR with a constant base can lead to incorrect offset separation. Suppose the address expression to begin with was.

where c is a compile time constant. 

%addr = base + scale * ( a ^ c )

The pass did the following 
%addr = base + ( (a ^ c) + disjoint(c)) * scale

This change allows it to compute the non-disjoint XOR to be computed correctly as follows. 

%addr =  base + ( ( a ^ non-disjoint(c) ) + disjoint(c) ) * scale. Where non-disjoint(c) = c & ~disjoint(c).

comments.author: pawan-nirpal-031
comments.body:
> Can you please explain why constants are problematic here? This looks like its papering over a bug somewhere else.

Following is a rough proof of what I think is going on, and yes I do agree that this will in a way mask the original issue. Here's how. 

We are trying to compute/simplify.

= b + scale * ( a ^ c ) 

But from the debugging in this pass I realized it's trying to compute the following in the transform. 

= b + ( (a ^ c) + disjoint(c)) * scale 

It should be 

= b + ( ( a ^ non-disjoint(c) ) + disjoint(c) ) * scale. 

Consider from the test case. 
a = 0 
c = 3 
scale = 4 

the expression should be 

= b + 4 * ( 0 ^ 3 ) = b + 12 

but the pass computes 
```DisjointBits = 3 & KnownZeros( 0 )``` 
= b + ((0 ^ 3)  + disjoint(3)) * 4 = b + (3 + 3) * 4 = b + 24 

What the pass ought to compute. non-disjoint(c) = c & ~disjoint(c) 
non-disjoint(3) = 3 & ~3 = 0
= b + ((0 ^ non-disjoint(3)) + disjoint(3)) * 4 
= b + ((0 ^ 0 ) + 3 ) * 4 = b + 3*4 = b + 12.

Apologies for a rough sketch.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Yes, that matches my understanding of how this transform is supposed to work. The only question is why for the case of a constant operand specifically we fail to adjust the other xor operand to the non-disjoint bits. I suspect it's related to this logic: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/ed36bff24662737c9c7a3b9ab3c0ecaf46587e42/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/SeparateConstOffsetFromGEP.cpp#L728-L733

comments.author: pawan-nirpal-031
comments.body:
> Yes, that matches my understanding of how this transform is supposed to work. The only question is why for the case of a constant operand specifically we fail to adjust the other xor operand to the non-disjoint bits. I suspect it's related to this logic:
> 
> https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/ed36bff24662737c9c7a3b9ab3c0ecaf46587e42/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/SeparateConstOffsetFromGEP.cpp#L728-L733

If I understand this correctly, I think this should not return the original xor but

a ^ non-disjoint(c).

Alive2 for tests 

https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/vtJ9nE
https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/Vg_WgB

comments.author: pawan-nirpal-031
comments.body: @nikic @arsenm @jrbyrnes @sgundapa ping for review. Apologies for multiple pings.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
Okay, I think I finally understand how this works. 

extractDisjointBitsFromXor() will push the non-disjoint bits to the UserChain: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/dba9397b3173bee1591830f2829abd5ef5538946/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/SeparateConstOffsetFromGEP.cpp#L827

Then distributeCastsAndCloneChain() will clone the instructions, using these checks to determine which operand is the chained one: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/dba9397b3173bee1591830f2829abd5ef5538946/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/SeparateConstOffsetFromGEP.cpp#L694-L696

Because we pushed a constant, which is not equal to a non-constant that is usually the xor LHS, this will pick OpNo=1 and end up inserting the non-disjoint bits into that operand.

Then removeConstOffset(), which is actually supposed to remove offsets, has special code to not do this, because it has already happened during initial cloning: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/dba9397b3173bee1591830f2829abd5ef5538946/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/SeparateConstOffsetFromGEP.cpp#L728-L733

Now, where this all goes wrong for this test case with a constant is the comparison in distributeCastsAndCloneChain. For the constant, the operands will actually be equal, so we pick the "wrong" operand and keep the original constant.

This is so hacky and convoluted that I'm inclined to just revert https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/150438 entirely.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: I think a proper fix can basically look like what you are doing but a) should not push to UserChain in extractDisjointBitsFromXor, b) maybe return the non-disjoint bits from it as well so we don't recompute them and c) the special Xor case in removeConstOffset() should always apply, not based on the value of NextInChain.

comments.author: pawan-nirpal-031

If these failures are unrelated to your changes (for example tests are broken or flaky at HEAD), please open an issue at https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues and add the `infrastructure` label.

comments.author: pawan-nirpal-031
comments.body:
Hi @nikic @arsenm It's becoming increasingly convoluted and complex try to fix the underlying issues of the pass, I think it's best to revert https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/150438 and revisit the design of the pass. If we are to revert the original PR, could you also be open to revert it in release branch. 

CC @efriedma-quic @sgundapa

comments.author: efriedma-quic
comments.body: I'd be fine with reverting for now (and cherry-picking the revert to the branch).

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Yes, I'd definitely prefer a revert. The way the xor handling was integrated in this pass was not good, and it's best to redo it from scratch.

comments.author: pawan-nirpal-031
comments.body: @nikic, @efriedma-quic thanks for the confirmation, since I do not have the write access, so I can't create a revert PR using Github UI, asking the author of the original PR @sgundapa  could you please?

comments.author: efriedma-quic
comments.body: From what I recall, you don't need commit access to click the "revert" button, but anyway, created #179339.

comments.author: pawan-nirpal-031
comments.body:
Closing this PR, Thanks @efriedma-quic for the revert PR. 

At the risk of moving off the topic, "you don't need commit access to click the "revert" button" I don't see a revert button If I recall correctly it is supposed to be at the end where the banner in purple says the PR has been merged. Anyways thanks.
